title: Retirement by Anne Bronte — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
O, let me be alone a while,
No human form is nigh.
And may I sing and muse aloud,
No mortal ear is by.
Away! ye dreams of earthly bliss,
Ye earthly cares begone:
Depart! ye restless wandering thoughts,
And let me be alone!

One hour, my spirit, stretch thy wings,
And quit this joyless sod,
Bask in the sunshine of the sky,
And be alone with God!
meaning: "Retirement" by Anne Bronte is a deep and nature poem consisting of 13 lines. The poet explores themes of deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "O, let me be alone a while, No human form is nigh....", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Anne Bronte's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
